meta.title: <Subject> — Cronologia
meta.subtitle: Chronology of <subject> (<years>)
meta.dataQualityNote: Work-in-progress dataset compiled from public sources (see references). Contested claims are attributed to their authors; dates not yet verified against a primary source are flagged with a ? mark. Corrections against primary sources are welcome.

events.title: <event with uncertain date>
events.text: <sources disagree — say how. Prose fields may cross-link the shared glossary with an inline marker, e.g. a [[latae-sententiae|latae sententiae]] penalty or plain [[schism]].>

figures.name: <name>
figures.role: <role, neutrally described; attribute any contested characterization>

organizations.name: <related organization>
organizations.relation: <relationship to the subject — including what it is NOT, for common confusions>

lineage.heading: Genealogy
lineage.note: <who consecrated/initiated whom — and which links are NOT lineage. Attribute contested validity/status characterizations to who says so and when.>

children.name: <direct successor>
children.status: <time-bounded status, attributed (e.g. 'X declared ... in YYYY; the group disputes it')>

children.name: <associated figure — NOT a successor>
children.detail: <the indirect link, attributed — renders with a DASHED connector>

trees.title: <separate line — no connection to the main line>
trees.summary: <included for disambiguation only; `separate: true` sets it apart visually>

branchTimeline.heading: Divisions at a glance
branchTimeline.note: <how the organization's divisions forked over time; contested labels ('schism', 'expulsion') attributed to who uses them>

branches.label: <first split>
branches.note: <who left / was expelled and why — attributed>

branches.label: <group that ended>
branches.note: <a branch with an `end` year (merged/reconciled/dissolved) renders a terminal dot>

branches.label: <split from a split>
branches.note: <`from` forks this branch off split-a's line instead of the trunk>

numbersChart.heading: How big is it? — contested numbers
numbersChart.note: <two different questions, two different methods — e.g. a movement counts its own participants while an external survey estimates a population share. Both bear on 'how big is it', but they are not the same measurement.>
numbersChart.unitNote: These series use different units and different methods (self-report vs. external survey). They are shown on separate axes and are NOT directly comparable — only their sources make either number meaningful. Contested numbers are never silently unified onto one scale.

series.label: <what this series measures — the movement's own estimate>
series.sourceLabel: <who reported it — e.g. the movement itself, self-report>

series.label: <what this series measures — an external survey>
series.sourceLabel: <who reported it — e.g. an independent survey organization>

points.display: ≈ half of the population, by survey estimate

items.title: <X ≠ Y>
items.text: <the distinction people flatten, stated with attribution — e.g. [[sedevacantism]] is not the same as [[schism]] (link ids must exist in data/glossary-terms.json)>
